{"query":"Supreme Court Texas and New Mexico nuclear waste storage case cert granted case name lower court 2024","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1800,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://www.pbs.org/newshour/politics/supreme-court-steps-into-fight-over-nuclear-waste-storage-in-rural-texas-and-new-mexico","title":"Supreme Court steps into fight over nuclear waste storage in rural Texas and New Mexico | PBS News","content":"WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court agreed on Friday to step into a fight over plans to store nuclear waste at sites in rural Texas and New Mexico.
Greg Abbott of Texas has said his state "will not become America's nuclear waste dumping ground."
The fight over storing nuclear waste is among 13 cases the justices added to their agenda for the term that begins Monday.
Only the 5th Circuit allowed the cases to proceed."},{"url":"https://www.ehn.org/supreme-court-clears-path-for-temporary-nuclear-waste-storage-in-texas-and-new-mexico","title":"Supreme Court clears path for temporary nuclear waste storage in Texas and New Mexico - EHN","content":"Supreme Court has allowed plans to move forward on storing nuclear waste at private sites in Texas and New Mexico, reversing a lower court’s decision that had blocked the effort.
The court’s 6-3 decision revives licenses granted by the Nuclear Regulatory Commission (NRC) to two companies to store radioactive waste at sites in rural Texas and New Mexico for up to 40 years, with a possible extension.
But placing temporary facilities in largely rural, lower-income areas raises deep concerns about environmental justice and long-term public health risks."},{"url":"https://supreme.justia.com/cases/federal/us/605/23-1300/","title":"Nuclear Regulatory Commission v. Texas | 605 U.S. ___ (2025) | Justia U.S. Supreme Court Center","content":"In September 2021, the NRC granted ISP a license to build and operate the storage facility.
More than 50 commercial nuclear power plants in the United States now store spent nuclear fuel on site at the plants themselves.
Eligible storage facilities can obtain renewable 40-year licenses for interim storage of spent nuclear fuel “pending its ultimate disposal.” Id., at 74694, 74703; 10 CFR §72.42.
The Nuclear Regulatory Commission and ISP both sought review in this Court, and we granted certiorari.
Justice Gorsuch, with whom Justice Thomas and Justice Alito join, dissenting.
Writing on behalf of the State, Governor Greg Abbott warned that storing spent fuel on “a concrete pad” in an oilfield containing more than 40% of America’s proven oil reserves would be “dangerous.” App.
480, 487 (2024) (internal quotation marks and ellipses omitted); accord, Yates v.
Brief amici curiae of United States Senator Ted Cruz, et al. filed.
Petition for a writ of certiorari filed.
(Response due July 12, 2024)"},{"url":"https://www.ans.org/news/2026-01-13/article-7671/supreme-court-nixes-beyond-nuclears-cisf-challenge/","title":"Supreme Court nixes Beyond Nuclear’s CISF challenge -- ANS / Nuclear Newswire","content":"January 13, 2026, 12:40PMRadwaste Solutions
Supreme Court has denied a petition by antinuclear group Beyond Nuclear challenging the Nuclear Regulatory Commission’s licensing of Holtec International’s proposed consolidated interim storage facility (CISF) for spent nuclear fuel in New Mexico.

The cases are consolidated, and a total of one hour is allotted for","score":0.75312227,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.oyez.org/cases/2024/23-1300","title":"Nuclear Regulatory Commission v. Texas","content":"*   Opinion of the Court (Kavanaugh). The U.S. Court of Appeals for the Fifth Circuit vacated the license, holding that neither the Atomic Energy Act nor the Nuclear Waste Policy Act authorized the Commission to license private interim storage facilities. A facility to store spent nuclear fuel at a private off-site location requires a license from the Nuclear Regulatory Commission, and only parties to the Commission’s licensing proceeding may obtain judicial review of the licensing decision under the Hobbs Act. Justice Brett Kavanaugh authored the 6-3 majority opinion of the Court. Justice Neil Gorsuch authored a dissenting opinion, joined by Justices Clarence Thomas and Samuel Alito, arguing that the Nuclear Waste Policy Act explicitly prohibits storage of spent nuclear fuel anywhere except at reactor sites or federally owned facilities, and that Texas and Fasken qualified as parties under the Hobbs Act because they participated in the environmental review portion of the NRC’s licensing proceeding.","score":0.5818027,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.ehn.org/supreme-court-clears-path-for-temporary-nuclear-waste-storage-in-texas-and-new-mexico","title":"Supreme Court clears path for temporary nuclear waste ...","content":"The U.S. Supreme Court has allowed plans to move forward on storing nuclear waste at private sites in Texas and New Mexico, reversing a lower","score":0.56517935,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.gwlr.org/nrc-v-texas","title":"Supreme Court Confirms Limits of Federal Court Jurisdiction Regarding Nuclear Waste in NRC v. Texas","content":"+ Annual Review of Administrative Law. Home > On The Docket > Oct. Term 2024 > Supreme Court Confirms Limits of Federal Court Jurisdiction Regarding Nuclear Waste in *NRC v. # Supreme Court Confirms Limits of Federal Court Jurisdiction Regarding Nuclear Waste in *NRC v. The U.S. Supreme Court held that the State of Texas and Fasken Land and Minerals Ltd. failed to become parties in the NRC administrative proceedings and therefore were not entitled to judicial review. At oral argument, Justice Kavanaugh had only one question and it was directed to the advocate for Texas. Given only actions of Texas, not the NRC, to explain why Texas had not tried to become a party to the NRC licensing proceeding, Justice Kavanaugh went on to write the Court’s majority opinion that held the Hobbs Act says what it says, and there’s nothing special about nuclear or nuclear waste that requires special rules for it.","score":0.53094244,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.pbs.org/newshour/politics/supreme-court-steps-into-fight-over-nuclear-waste-storage-in-rural-texas-and-new-mexico","title":"Supreme Court steps into fight over nuclear waste storage ...","content":"The Supreme Court agreed on Friday to step into a fight over plans to store nuclear waste at sites in rural Texas and New Mexico.","score":0.51822984,"raw_content":null}],"response_time":1.46,"request_id":"c7712979-239b-4a6a-93b7-b7b8ca650fa4"}
